summary:
Glycine plays a fundamental role by ensuring accurate protein structure formation early during synthesis; while hydroxyproline contributes importantly through post-synthetic modification steps that strengthen bonds within collagens' triple helices but does not initiate their inherent stability directly. Collagen’s structural integrity fundamentally relies upon initial precise assembly facilitated largely by glycine residues throughout its chain lengthening phase prior to further stabilizing interactions involving prolyl-4-hydroxylase activity among others. To clarify any remaining doubts about these core mechanisms,
To reiterate succinctly: Glycine forms essential backbone framework enabling proper alignment which then allows secondary/tertiary level reinforcements from additional cross-linking events mediated mainly by enzymes such as lysyl oxidases or procollagen C-protein peptidase activities later enhancing overall tensile properties without altering primary sequence coding itself significantly impacting final mechanical performance characteristics observed clinically across various tissues relying heavily on intact collagen networks including skin ligaments tendons etc.. This summary encapsulates both aspects clearly addressing original query regarding what makes up basic building blocks versus those adding complexity after factoring into ultimate physical attributes
